cover-letter-mistakes-that-get-you-rejec.title: Top Cover Letter Mistakes That Get You Rejected
cover-letter-mistakes-that-get-you-rejec.description: Avoid these common cover letter mistakes that lead to rejection. Learn actionable tips and use ShipMicro's tool to create a standout application.

sections.p: One of the most frequent cover letter mistakes that get you rejected is sending a generic template without customization. Recruiters can spot boilerplate content instantly—a CareerBuilder survey found that 63% of hiring managers reject applications with generic cover letters. Your letter should specifically address the company, role, and how your skills match their needs. Mention the hiring manager by name if possible, reference the company's recent projects, and explain why you're excited about this particular opportunity. Avoid vague phrases like 'I'm a hard worker'—instead, provide concrete examples of achievements. ShipMicro's Cover Letter tool helps you tailor each letter with company-specific insights and keyword optimization to show genuine interest.

Another critical error is making your cover letter all about your needs rather than the employer's. Hiring managers want to know what you can do for them, not just what you want from the job. A study by TalentWorks revealed that cover letters emphasizing company benefits over personal goals have a 40% higher response rate. Instead of saying 'I want to grow my skills,' frame it as 'I will apply my expertise in X to help your team achieve Y.' Highlight how you'll solve their problems, improve efficiency, or contribute to specific goals. Use the ShipMicro tool to structure your content around the employer's pain points, ensuring your letter demonstrates value and alignment with their mission.

Simple typos and grammatical mistakes are among the top cover letter errors that lead to immediate rejection. According to a recent report, 75% of recruiters dismiss candidates after spotting just one or two spelling errors. These errors signal carelessness and lack of attention to detail—qualities no employer wants. Always proofread meticulously, use tools like Grammarly, and have a friend review your letter. Pay special attention to company names, job titles, and dates. ShipMicro's Cover Letter tool includes built-in grammar and spell-check features, plus AI-powered suggestions to polish your writing, ensuring your application is professional and error-free.

sections.h2: Being Too Long or Too Vague
sections.p: Lengthy or vague cover letters often get rejected because recruiters spend an average of just 7 seconds scanning applications. Aim for 250-400 words—concise yet impactful. Avoid rambling introductions or repeating your resume; instead, focus on 2-3 key achievements that directly relate to the job. Use specific metrics, like 'increased sales by 20%' rather than 'improved sales.' Structure your letter with clear paragraphs: an engaging opening, a middle highlighting relevant experience, and a strong closing call to action. ShipMicro's tool helps you optimize length and clarity with templates that prioritize brevity and impact, keeping recruiters engaged from start to finish.

Failing to include relevant keywords is a subtle but costly cover letter mistake that can get you rejected, especially with Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) screening up to 75% of applications. These systems scan for keywords from the job description, such as specific skills, software, or certifications. Without them, your letter may never reach a human. Carefully review the job posting and incorporate key terms naturally—for example, if the role requires 'project management,' mention your experience with it. ShipMicro's Cover Letter tool analyzes job descriptions to suggest optimal keywords, ensuring your letter passes ATS filters and appeals to hiring managers, boosting your chances of landing an interview.
